features.title: pi-jit-agents consumer migration arc
features.description: Migrate pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors to import agent infrastructure from @davidorex/pi-jit-agents. Resolve the provider-pin, thinking-seam, and cross-consumer duplication at the correct boundary (pi-jit-agents executeAgent). Close the cluster of open issues that are blocked on this migration: 043 (duplicate agent infrastructure), 045 (framework-level anti-injection), 048 (agent discovery contradiction), 049 (step-loop cwd bug), 050 (monitor classify wiring).
features.motivation: The platonic first enactment of pi-jit-agents landed in commit 239f718 but consumers still carry their own copies of agent infrastructure. This duplication is load-bearing: the model-pin that breaks monitors under OpenRouter-only auth lives inside a consumer copy of parseModelSpec, not at the framework boundary. Fixing it in the consumer creates throwaway work since the code is about to move. Fixing it at the correct boundary (executeAgent in pi-jit-agents) resolves the pin and the duplication in one arc. The arc is also the operational-pattern test case for the Muni five-layer model enacted against PLAN-001 — how it plays out here informs what pi-project must eventually generate natively per the framework gaps in FGAP-001 through FGAP-006.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0001, ADR-0002, ADR-0003 all advance to decided state via user-authored transitions
parseModelSpec lives in pi-jit-agents at the execute boundary; pi-behavior-monitors' copy is removed
executeAgent enforces the thinking-seam policy per ADR-0002 with explicit warning
pi-behavior-monitors' classifyViaAgent delegates to pi-jit-agents executeAgent, carrying no duplicate agent-spec or template code
pi-workflows' agent-step dispatch delegates to pi-jit-agents executeAgent, removing step-shared.ts and local agent-spec/template helpers
All five bundled classifier YAMLs in pi-behavior-monitors/agents/ align with the decided ADR-0001 and ADR-0002 policies
Issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 close or migrate to scope-owned registries
Empirical verification: hedge monitor runs successfully against an OpenRouter-routed Claude model
Empirical verification: hedge monitor runs successfully against at least one Kimi model (via openrouter or via kimi-coding direct)
All tests in npm test remain at 0 failures
CI passes on Node 22/23

stories.title: Resolve model-pin policy and apply at pi-jit-agents
stories.description: Apply the ADR-0001 decision by implementing bare-model-id resolution through ExtensionContext at the pi-jit-agents execute boundary. Remove the anthropic-default pin from the current parseModelSpec implementation in pi-behavior-monitors.
acceptance_criteria: A bare model id in an agent spec resolves against the current session's provider via ExtensionContext lookup, not a hardcoded default
If ExtensionContext cannot supply currentModel, dispatch errors with a clear message naming the missing binding
Fully-qualified provider/model specs continue to pin explicitly
Unit tests cover: bare-id + session-with-model, bare-id + session-without-model (error), fully-qualified-id (pinned)

tasks.title: Verify ExtensionContext exposes currentModel
tasks.description: Inspect pi-agent-core's ExtensionContext type to confirm whether currentModel (or equivalent) is available to extensions at dispatch time. If not, the task widens into a prerequisite against pi-agent-core — escalate to user for scope decision.
tasks.acceptance: Written confirmation in the task record of what ExtensionContext currently exposes and whether ADR-0001's chosen option is implementable today or depends on upstream changes

tasks.title: Implement bare-id resolution in pi-jit-agents
tasks.description: Add model-resolution logic at executeAgent. Bare ids trigger ExtensionContext lookup; fully-qualified specs pin.
tasks.acceptance: executeAgent resolves bare ids via ExtensionContext, surfaces clear error on missing binding, preserves fully-qualified semantics

tasks.title: Unit tests for model resolution
tasks.acceptance: Three-way coverage (bare + session, bare + no session, fully-qualified) all passing

stories.title: Apply thinking-seam enforcement in pi-jit-agents
stories.description: Apply the ADR-0002 decision: executeAgent enforces thinking=off when forced toolChoice is present, emits a warning naming the agent and the incompatibility.
acceptance_criteria: executeAgent inspects dispatch options for forced toolChoice
When forced toolChoice is present and compiled spec declares thinking: on, thinking is disabled and a warning is emitted via a structured diagnostic channel
Non-forced dispatches continue to honor the spec's thinking field
Unit test covers: forced-toolChoice + thinking:on (warning + thinking off), forced-toolChoice + thinking:off (no warning), free-form + thinking:on (thinking forwarded)

tasks.title: Add pre-dispatch check in executeAgent
tasks.acceptance: Pre-dispatch branch inspects toolChoice and compiled spec's thinking field; when incompatible, disables thinking and emits warning

tasks.title: Define structured diagnostic channel
tasks.description: Decide whether warnings emit via logger, alongside JitAgentResult, or both. Must be consumable by both pi-workflows (workflow steps) and pi-behavior-monitors (classify calls).
tasks.acceptance: JitAgentResult gains a diagnostics field or equivalent; both consumers can read it

stories.title: Move parseModelSpec to pi-jit-agents
stories.description: Execute the ADR-0003 decision: move parseModelSpec (or its renamed successor) from pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts into pi-jit-agents at the execute boundary. Remove the duplicate from the consumer. Update the classifyViaAgent call site to use the new location.
acceptance_criteria: parseModelSpec exported from pi-jit-agents at a named subpath (e.g. @davidorex/pi-jit-agents/runtime)
packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1091-1097 removed
classifyViaAgent imports and uses the pi-jit-agents version
No anthropic-default fallback anywhere in the codebase for bare model ids
Unit tests from STORY-001 continue to pass

tasks.title: Export parseModelSpec from pi-jit-agents
tasks.acceptance: Function available at a named subpath; visible in pi-jit-agents' exports

tasks.title: Remove duplicate from pi-behavior-monitors
tasks.acceptance: Lines 1091-1097 removed; classifyViaAgent imports from pi-jit-agents

tasks.title: Audit pi-workflows for equivalent parsing
tasks.description: Search pi-workflows/src for any model-id parsing or provider-selection logic. If found, consolidate into pi-jit-agents too.
tasks.acceptance: Written audit note in this task record; any duplicates consolidated or explicitly flagged as out-of-scope with justification

stories.title: Migrate pi-workflows agent infrastructure to pi-jit-agents
stories.description: Replace pi-workflows' local copies of agent-spec.ts, template.ts, and step-shared.ts agent helpers with imports from pi-jit-agents. Consumer call sites (step-agent.ts, dispatch.ts) delegate to executeAgent.
acceptance_criteria: packages/pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.ts either removed or reduced to a re-export from pi-jit-agents
packages/pi-workflows/src/template.ts equivalently reduced
packages/pi-workflows/src/step-shared.ts' agent-helper portions migrated; any non-agent helpers remain
Workflow agent steps execute end-to-end through pi-jit-agents executeAgent
Existing tsx --test suite for pi-workflows passes
No circular dependency introduced between pi-workflows and pi-jit-agents

tasks.title: Map pi-workflows agent-infrastructure surface to pi-jit-agents equivalents
tasks.acceptance: Written mapping in this task of every symbol in pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.ts, template.ts, step-shared.ts to its pi-jit-agents target; any unmappable symbols flagged for separate handling

tasks.title: Migrate call sites in pi-workflows step executors
Update pi-workflows package.json dependencies
tasks.acceptance: @davidorex/pi-jit-agents listed as a direct dependency with concrete caret range

tasks.title: Run pi-workflows test suite
tasks.acceptance: npm test passes for pi-workflows at 0 failures

stories.title: Migrate pi-behavior-monitors classifyViaAgent to pi-jit-agents
stories.description: Rewrite classifyViaAgent to delegate fully to pi-jit-agents executeAgent. Remove local imports of pi-workflows agent helpers (issue-043 duplication) and local agent-spec/template code that pi-jit-agents now owns.
acceptance_criteria: classifyViaAgent contains no agent-spec parsing, no template rendering, no parseModelSpec — only collector wiring, template-context assembly, and a call to pi-jit-agents executeAgent
pi-behavior-monitors no longer imports anything from pi-workflows
pi-behavior-monitors package.json lists @davidorex/pi-jit-agents as a direct dependency, not pi-workflows
Existing vitest suite for pi-behavior-monitors passes

tasks.title: Run pi-behavior-monitors test suite
tasks.acceptance: npx vitest run passes for pi-behavior-monitors at 0 failures

stories.title: Align bundled classifier YAMLs with decided policies
stories.description: Update all five bundled classifier agent YAMLs (hedge, commit-hygiene, fragility, work-quality, unauthorized-action) to conform to the ADR-0001 model-field semantics and ADR-0002 thinking-seam policy.
acceptance_criteria: Every bundled classifier YAML's model field is consistent with ADR-0001 — either a bare id that resolves via ExtensionContext, or a fully-qualified provider/model, depending on the final decision
Every bundled classifier YAML's thinking field is set to off (or removed if the default is off) per ADR-0002
No YAML contains dead config that the classify path silently ignores

stories.title: Remove duplicate schema copies per issue-043
stories.description: Consolidate duplicate copies of verdict.schema.json and any other schema fragments that pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors, and pi-jit-agents currently carry independently. Canonical owner is pi-jit-agents; consumers import.
acceptance_criteria: verdict.schema.json exists only in pi-jit-agents
Any other schema fragments duplicated across consumers are consolidated to their correct owner package
Consumers import schemas via the owner package's subpath exports
Issue 043 closes

tasks.title: Identify duplicate schema copies
tasks.description: Grep all packages/*/schemas and packages/*/src for *.schema.json files. Produce a duplicate inventory.
tasks.acceptance: Written inventory listing every duplicate and its canonical owner

tasks.title: Update imports to use canonical owner

stories.title: Empirical verification against OpenRouter and Kimi
stories.description: Run the hedge monitor against an OpenRouter-routed Claude model (openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6) and against at least one Kimi model to verify that forced toolChoice works end-to-end through the openai-completions path. This is the §7/§9 caveat from analysis/openrouter-pi-mono-setup.md — plausibly works but not documented. If it fails, surface as a blocking finding for user decision.
acceptance_criteria: hedge monitor classify returns a VERDICT_TOOL tool call from openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6
hedge monitor classify returns a VERDICT_TOOL tool call from at least one Kimi model (openrouter/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking or kimi-coding/kimi-k2-thinking)
If either verification fails, a new finding is created in spec-reviews.json or as a defect in this feature's findings array, and the arc pauses for user decision

tasks.title: Run hedge against openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6
tasks.acceptance: Classify call returns a valid verdict; failure recorded as a finding if not

tasks.title: Run hedge against a Kimi model
tasks.acceptance: Classify call returns a valid verdict; failure recorded as a finding if not

tasks.title: Document verification outcomes
tasks.description: Write outcomes into this feature's findings array or into a new finding in REVIEW-001 if the outcome surfaces a spec-level disclarity

stories.title: Close or migrate resolved issues
stories.description: Update or migrate issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 in issues.json to reflect their closure by this arc. Each issue either marks resolved with a reference to the relevant STORY/TASK, or migrates to a scope-owned finding registry per the PHASE-6 retroactive migration.
acceptance_criteria: issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 each have status resolved (or have been migrated to a scope-owned registry with the same terminal state)
Each resolved issue has resolved_by pointing to the commit or feature that closed it
